extra.reasoning:
The property requires globally (opted_out implies not sent), i.e., no reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true. The transition [t_resume_glitch] on send_attempt if (opted_out and ticks >= 10): sent := true directly sets sent=true while opted_out is true, which violates the property whenever its guard is satisfiable.

The guard requires opted_out=true and ticks>=10, both reachable. Note that when opted_out=true and ticks>=10, both [t_send_blocked] (guard opted_out) and [t_resume_glitch] (guard opted_out and ticks>=10) are enabled on send_attempt; the spec gives no priority ordering, so an execution may legitimately take t_resume_glitch. Since the property must hold on every reachable execution, this branch witnesses a violation.

Trace from initial state (opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0):
- tick x10: t_tick increments ticks (capped at 10) and sets sent=false each time. Result: opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=10.
- optout: t_optout sets opted_out=true, sent=false. State: opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=10.
- send_attempt: guard of t_resume_glitch = (opted_out and ticks>=10) = (true and 10>=10) = true, so sent:=true. State: opted_out=true, sent=true, ticks=10.

This reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true, violating globally(opted_out implies not sent). The execution is 12 events, within the 14-event bound. Hence the property is violated.
